reviews.review_text: Excellent place to stay for a short vacation with friends in rural Victoria.
Clean and fabulous room! Just next to the canyon walk. Beautiful scenes around the area. Will come back again next time for sure!

Spacious site, clean bathroom, speedy wifi, and absolute beautiful camp kitchen.
Can’t complain.

Great park with great staff. Very clean and quiet and closest park to town just a 5 minute walk. Alex the manager was very friendly and helpful and always there if we needed him. Coffee is amazing at the little coffee van and the grounds were extremely well manicured. Quite expensive to stay here but very well worth it. You get what you pay for with caravan parks and this one did not disappoint. 5 stars to Alex and his great team, and Tasman parks.
Alex and the staff did an amazing job making our holiday fun, safe and memorable. We will definitely be back!
Rooms had a few issues, has stove wouldn't lite, door on main bedroom wouldn't close.

Walking distance to shops /Pubs /super market |Clean site |Clean toilet |Clean shower (Air Conditioning)|Quite area |Swimming lake close by .|Great for kids (Highly recommend) this area for kids.water park that is .

We loved our stay in the Alpine Villa, it was cosy and clean. Bev at front reception was so welcoming. I would highly recommend staying here. We stayed over Australia Day weekend and enjoyed the convenience of being able to walk everywhere.

LUXURY CABIN WITH TERMITES IN BED

Checked in around 3pm we noticed the bed sheets were a bit dirty (some marks and some hair) on it. The older lady at reception was kind enough to change the top sheet for us after we showed her.

Later that evening around 9:30pm we were in bed watching tv when we started to get itchy and noticed little bugs throughout the mattress. We got up quite distressed and I killed 4 of them using toilet paper. Not long after we realised they were coming from many places around the bed however we did not notice any in the living area, sofa or anywhere else in the cabin. There were termites throughout the luxury cabin bed. We called the after hours number and the gentleman that attended (Fion) was kind enough to have a look and admit that it was far from ideal, suggesting we move into an alternative cabin asap.

At 11pm that night we had to pack everything away and set up in the new cabin. Noting the new cabin does not have the luxury features of our previous cabin that we paid for.

Reception next day advised that we would be compensated a full night accommodation as we paid $350 per night. However later on, retracted this claim and only refunded $100. Terrible service and not what you expect in a $350 p/n luxury cabin. DO NOT GO TO THIS HOLIDAY PARK. To be told they were flying ants when there were none anywhere else within the cabin and knowing we kept the door closed the whole night was quite condescending.
We r staying in villa name Fitzgerald rooms r tidy and clean Best Location for holidays thanks to All staff.

Booked big4 because of high rating. However I found a few disadvantages:
- Shared laundry with extra cost
- Very small room size
- Limited kids facilities

In all of my travels, this would have to be the best, cleanest, most modern and well set up holiday park I’ve ever stayed at. We stayed in one of the cabins however even the caravan sites were well set up and spacious. The staff were very nice and helpful and there’s even an on-site coffee van! I highly recommend this holiday park!

I had such a wonderful stay at BIG4 Tasman Holiday Park Bright! The place is beautiful, peaceful, and perfectly located for a relaxing getaway. The staff were so friendly and welcoming , they even surprised me with a bottle of sparkling wine to celebrate my birthday! 🥂 Such a thoughtful touch that made my day extra special. Everything was clean and well-maintained, and the atmosphere was just amazing. Highly recommend this place to anyone visiting Bright! 💕🫶🏻

A very small walk to town, amazing price, clean cabins with plenty provided.
Friendly staff and dog friendly cabins.
Couldn't fault it and we will be back!

What a fantastic place to stay.
We got the newly renovated cabins and they were supremely comfortable. The furnishings and the beds are first rate.
I highly recommend this as a place to stay if it’s for work or with the family. The location is second to none and there’s a coffee caravan on site!!

We stayed in the luxury 1 bed cabin for 2 nights and we were impressed by the dark finishes and quality.  Bathroom was lovely and spacious shower. No expense spared!Really cosy & warm cabin.
Definitely return 1 day.
Vince D’Argenio
Loved this
had a lovely time

Stayed in a recently renovated treetop 2 bedroom cabin. Very neat, clean and cosy. Well appointed and functional. Great location only a stones throw from the brewery, the river and the large playground, which is great when holidaying with kids. The coffee van makes great coffees and the staff were very friendly. Ever so slightly on the pricier end for caravan park cabins, but you do get what you pay for.
Cozy, clean rooms and a well-laid-out, spotless park. Easy walking distance to the centre of town. Perfect place to stay in Bright!
Loved our last minute weekend getaway. The villa was very nice definitely will stay again and highly recommend. Located in a great area near shops and restaurants.

A good location, nice room, safe and privacy.
Very good place make up vacation with family, friends and community groups activities.

I’ve stayed here twice already when I went skiing with my friends, and every time it truly feels like a home away from home. The place is incredibly comfortable and convenient — everything you need is right there. It’s definitely one of the best accommodations I’ve chosen when visiting Bright. I’ll always come back here whenever I get the chance!

Gather around people and let me tell you the story of one of, if not the best caravan park I've stayed in. It all started with a simple request to check in early, not only did they grant this request but I also received a text stating that the cabin was ready and I could check in. Upon arrival staff welcomed me with massive smiles and a friendly discussion plus some activities for the children. I asked about the best place to hire snow chains, look further the staff had me sorted again and within a couple of minutes I was getting shown how to fit them. The park is extremely family friendly and the cabins very nice and cosy. A short walk and you're in the heart of Bright where there's heaps to do. Amazing staff and amazing park, will definitely be back and highly recommend.

Fabulous Cabins...very spacious, well equipped and homely. Excellent location to town.
Newly renovated cabins with extra touches to make it feel homely.
Jess did mention a very kind deed in my time of need - thank you to such wonderful staff.
Ramai

We stayed here for three nights and were warmly welcomed by the friendly staff, who went above and beyond to ensure our stay was exceptional. The amenities were clean, and the grounds were well-maintained. The staff made sure the kids had a great time. Unfortunately, our dog blankets went missing from the laundry area, but the team was incredibly helpful, ensuring our pups stayed warm throughout the night. The property also features a café, and we look forward to returning for another visit—this time to enjoy a coffee!

Great location.
Very clean. Great service

Went to stay at Bright a couple of months ago and Bev who helped with the check in process upgraded us based on the booking availability that opened up during the time of check in without even asking! Then the team offered us a late check out at a marginal extra cost which fit perfectly with our schedule. Loved the whole experience and hospitality!
Great location, very clean cabins, awesome facilities. Staff were super friendly and the coffee van was a bonus!

Stayed in cabin for 3 nights with family during core winter and it was great experience. Amenities in kitchen, bedrooms,  bathrooms and common areas were there and was child friendly. With smart tv and firewood log display heater, it was perfect and also seems like a nice upgrade to these cabins recently. Staff was very helpful too with custom requests. Bright is my top big4 family visits so will definitely go there again!

Friendly staff on arrival. Super clean cabin with superb linen. The best bed I've slept in for along time. Well stocked cabin with a lot of thought put into
practicality and comfort. Warm toasty room on arrival. Beautiful grounds within a few hundred metres of town.
Had an amazing time at the property. The park is clean, peaceful, and in a beautiful spot. Bev was so sweet and helpful—she truly made our stay special. Highly recommend and would love to come back!

We recently stayed at Bright Big 4 as we do for peace & quiet.|Accom. was tired & in need of an overhall & cleaning. |Rickerty balcony fence, |holey fly wire screen on front door, dusty skirting boards, fluff on couch & floors, uncleaned toaster, fingerprints on electric kettle, badly scratched frypan, not lighter for gas stove, very mouldy shower base. |Not impressed this time. |No former knowledge of excavation & landscaping each day from 8am till 5pm. Within metres of our cabin. Very noisy & dusty.|If we had of been informed we would have changed accomm. |There was no mention of compensation on leaving. |From now on, we will have to seriously consider Big 4. |
地理位置优越、走路可以到小镇、环境幽雅。可能是季节原因、星期天有电桩的位置要99元澳币、还是应该来一次

I cannot recommend this holiday park more highly. The staff are very friendly and helpful, the cleanliness is high, the coffee caravan is amazing and the proximity to town is excellent and very manageable for small children.
The best caravan park in Bright.  Being very close to the Bright shopping and everything Bright has to offer is a bonus.  We have been coming here for many years.
It’s a great site and the amenities are of a high standard.  Very close the main town.  I would come back for sure.
Lovely

Surge Pricing gone mad.|We have enjoyed our caravan stay in this park many times. We were going to enjoy the park again but we tried  to book for 3 nights  recently for a powered site The price was $470 and the owners need to wake up or loose our business going forward .
One of the nicest sites in an very nice part of Australia
What a lovely place to visit. The cabins were lovely and the staff were great. Will definitely be back again one day.
Great spot, very nice caravan park in great location only a few hundred metres to the edge of town, park nextdoor and popular swimming spot, Bright is wonderful!
Quite easy walking distance to the town
Friendly staff, good and really clean amenities and well looked after park. Would definitely stay again

Beautiful park, lovely cabins, easily walk to the splash park and down the main street. The feel of the park is gorgeous, shady, green, trees and vines and fairly lights at night. Perfect. Staff are helpful and friendly. Love it.
It's good for the price. Very convenient location. Clean.
Dog friendly camping site. The location is perfect, next to the splash park. Luxury cabins are also available here. The cafe van provides nice coffee and pastries.

While it's in a nice spot and has lovely facilities, the sites are very small and the park is incredibly overcrowded.  If you don't mind being in the face of your neighbours then you'll love this park.  If you prefer a tiny bit of space then avoid this park.
Didn't have a room, had an ensuite site for my campervan. Love this park, close to everything and lovely staff.

$90 for a gravel site I could not fit my caravan and car what a joke. This park is being converted to a cabin park and are not interested in campers or caravaners. Try nrma around the corner, $35 a night on a proper site.

We love it here!
A beautiful small park, quiet, gorgeous trees and along the river. it has great amenities, a fantastic sky deck lounge and well kept camp kitchen and is walking distance to town. Very pet friendly. Bev on reception is the best customer service person we have met. she actually rang us to see how far away we were and waited for us to check in when we arrived after closing  and still took time to tell us about the park and town. it is one of the best parks we have stayed, without a doubt. There are some gorgeous looking cabins and not as many van sites. We have a MH and were given a great shady site on the river. Many caravaners were complaining about the high fees.  $89 p/n but goes up to $129 for a powered sire on Fri & Sat. This is off season! Whilst we loved the park this a bit too high when most of us are self sufficient with showers and toilets. There are discounts for Big4 members. I hope they review pricing and look after caravaners. We are much less maintenance than a cabin and would love to come back for longer over summer.  Many thanks to Bev, much appreciated!

We booked a 2 night stay on a caravan site at this Park and are so glad we did!! It was warm when we arrived to our lovely, level site backing onto the river and it is the first time we have encountered an air conditioned laundry which was much appreciated. Next morning the delight continued when we discovered the toilet and shower block was heated on what proved to be a chilly morning. The Park is very quiet and all the staff are top notch - thank you for a great stay 😊
These folks have invested heavily to make these facilities best in market. The owner walks the grounds helping and being available to get you to your cabin. Amazing location.
Great location

We booked the” pet friendly HOTHAM cabin” which was not pet friendly at all as we couldn’t leave our furry buddy unattended in the cabin . We have stayed at other BIG4 pet friendly locations and all of them let us leave our pet when we needed to go out. We thought it’s the rule of all BIG4 locations but this time we had to take him everywhere and we couldn’t enjoy some of the things we planned as a family. We really wanted to go out and eat but it was always a cabin meal which was really disappointing.|Also, the rooms were really small, not enough cupboard space in the main bedroom as there were too many blankets stored in them and no cupboards in the children’s bedroom. We had to keep our bags on the floor and walked on them as there was no walking space in the rooms.|Bunk bed was really unsafe with the ladder in a very awkward position so my child had to climb on to the single bed to get to the top and in between beds the heater was located. We booked for this cabin paying for 3 adults and 1 child however, the living had a very small sofa which is tightly fitted with the dining table and a very small outdated kitchen. We stayed at the BIG4 Ballarat windmill park the weekend before which was unbelievably great with excellent customer service but this time staff didn’t look happy and unhelpful.|Location was pretty close to everything but no point as we couldn’t enjoy most of the activities as a family due to their NOT PET FRIENDLY POLICY.|Will not stay there again as no member benefits and facilities like the other BIG4 locations especially due to UNFRIENDLINESS

We booked the” pet friendly HOTHAM cabin” which was not pet friendly at all as we couldn’t leave our furry buddy unattended in the cabin . We have stayed at other BIG4 pet friendly locations and all of them let us leave our pet when we needed to go out. We thought it’s the rule of all BIG4 locations but this time we had to take him everywhere and we couldn’t enjoy some of the things we planned as a family. We really wanted to go out and eat but it was always a cabin meal which was really disappointing.
Also, the rooms were really small, not enough cupboard space in the main bedroom as there were too many blankets stored in them and no cupboards in the children’s bedroom. We had to keep our bags on the floor and walked on them as there was no walking space in the rooms.
Bunk bed was really unsafe with the ladder in a very awkward position so my child had to climb on to the single bed to get to the top and in between beds the heater was located. We booked for this cabin paying for 3 adults and 1 child however, the living had a very small sofa which is tightly fitted with the dining table and a very small outdated kitchen. We stayed at the BIG4 Ballarat windmill park the weekend before which was unbelievably great with excellent customer service but this time staff didn’t look happy and unhelpful.
Location was pretty close to everything but no point as we couldn’t enjoy most of the activities as a family due to their NOT PET FRIENDLY POLICY.
Will not stay there again as no member benefits and facilities like the other BIG4 locations especially due to UNFRIENDLINESS

The staff were extremely accommodating and friendly. Facilities were excellent and the camp itself is beautifully well kept. ||We were traveling through with plans to camp in the Buckland Valley but the camp grounds were closed. This allowed us a chance to catch up on washing, have nice hot showers and a warm bed before heading back to camping. ||Definitely recommend and love that they don’t demand a 2 night minimum stay

Such a stunning caravan park, and perfectly located (just a short drive to the river and town). Nice townhouse  with a view of mountain from the front balcony so we really enjoyed our stay. The bed was comfy, the kitchen was very functional.

Nice cabin very close to town with a view of the Ovens River from the front balcony. I will say it was an absolute pleasure to have Bev as our welcome and of course an information guide for the area. Will be back for sure. Thanks 👌👍

Absolutely loved our stay at this hotel, which is a hidden gem for families, providing a perfect blend of comfort and fun.

The villa was spacious, clean, and beautifully decorated, offering stunning views of the surrounding landscape.

The staff went above and beyond to ensure we felt welcome, making us feel like part of their family.

We enjoyed quality family time while making unforgettable memories. Highly recommend for any family looking for a delightful getaway!

If you are planning for a day at the Mount Hotham Alpine Resort, this place is the best!

I booked a three-bedroom spa townhouse, and overall, it was a great experience. The view of the mountains was stunning, and the place was clean, quiet, and well-serviced. The kitchen was fully equipped with all necessary utensils, and there was a baby gate for added safety. The only drawback was the poor soundproofing; we could hear noises from the adjacent rooms quite clearly. Despite this, everything else was excellent, and we had a wonderful stay.
Nice cabin

Pretty average accommodation overall. We found the place to be quite dirty, upon arriving there were many cobwebs & every day after walking on the floors our socks would be brown & grotty. Poor form for the price per night.

This is a great caravan park that’s more like an upmarket motel. I stayed in one of the rooms, it was very clean and comfortable. Great coffee van open all day. Short walk to town. Highly recommended whether you have a van or staying in one of their units
Accommodation was lovely but checking in after hours with the map supplied was difficult. Signage was confusing. I'd definitely stay again.

Staff amazing and very nice
Rooms beautiful and clean
Location great holiday area close to the township

This was fantastic, so enjoyed our stay in Cope Hut, which is a studio apartment part of the old Bright Brewery.  The bed was super comfy, the kitchen was very functional, and maybe another couch to sit on would be great especially if you have a bad back. The staff were very friendly and so accommodating.  We enjoyed ourselves so much that we have already made a tentative booking for next year around the same time.  So see you next year Bright. I am already counting down.
Great location tidy spot no campfire smoke great coffee van stayed in caravan

Booked here for 4 nights and were given a brick unit.  Nice and roomy, beds hard but had electric blankets which was great.  All the taps needed new washers so leaked and then the shower cubicle leaked all over the bathroom floor.  It desperately needed a refurbishment of the bathroom and kitchen.  We were charged top price for this miserable unit and then were told it was getting redone after we left.  Not happy.  Should have had a discount for putting up with this.   As Tasman parks recently bought this place I thought they could be kinder to a G'Day member to encourage more business.  Won't be looking to book at Tasman parks in future.  We looked at a fully reconditioned unit next door which was wonderful and I would expect to pay the price we did for such a nice unit.

A quiet, small park. Located a 5 min stroll from town. Clean, modern facilities. Not on the river, no access. But a short walk away.
I do think the family bathroom needs its own door code to prevent individuals going in and taking lengthy showers, defeats the purpose
of have a family room. Also missing was a baby change table. Great heated indoor multipurpose room with games and movies for kids. Bathrooms were heated which was amazing.
We were left stranded on a Sunday night. No duty manager - lockbox didn’t work so couldn’t access our room. Absolute nightmare. Do not recommend.
Stayed one night, lovely and clean, great location and a friendly environment. Easy check in and checkout with a carapace for all cabins. Recommend a stay.
Gr8 place to stay

We stayed 5 nights in one of the 3 bedroom villas.

The good:
- location of the villa was terrific in that there was spectacular views of the beautiful autumn trees. Gorgeous!
- although the park is not right on the river, the location of the park is close to town and is walkable to town, and is close to the river.
- friendly staff, generally speaking.

The not so good:
- parking arrangements. The info on the website and printed info when checking in states one vehicle per unit/site, and yet during our stay multiple vacationers would have 2 cars or even 3! This results in cars being parked where they’re not supposed to be which make it extremely difficult to get in and out of your own parking spot, not to mention makes the park look really cluttered and spoils the views. On one occasion we called reception as another vacationer’s car was parked in front of their unit, not in their designated spot as they already had 1 car parked there, which resulted in getting our car in and out from our spot extremely difficult. It was confirmed that it’s one car per unit and the receptionist said she’d ask them to move their car to outside of the park. What eventuated was the people moved their car a few feet forward, but not out of the park. It was still really difficult to manoeuvre to get to our spot.

I don’t know whether the receptionist didn’t want to rock the boat with these people or something was lost in communication, but it’s not good enough. You either have the rule of one car pet unit and you monitor and enforce this, or you don’t. If you do want to enforce it, you guys should consider putting in a boom gate where each unit/site is given a PIN code to be able to enter and exit with one vehicle only in order to stop multiple cars and visitors parking their cars all over the place and effecting the car parking for those doing the right thing!
- no dishwasher in the villa, where for the nightly price paid for the self contained villa I kind of expected one would’ve been there.
- there was pretty much everything else needed in the kitchen area except for a grater. That would come in handy.
- the main bedroom has electric blankets on the bed but these weren’t plugged in, and the bed base was sooo heavy and couldn’t be moved in order to plug the electric blankets in. Essentially another easily reachable power point near the bed head is needed to be able to plug things in like chargers, electric blankets etc.
- whilst the linen and sheets were laundered clean, there were a couple of long strands of hair left on one of the towels and on the double/queen bed blanket in that room, I’m guessing from when the bed was made and the towel placed there when the room was made up between different guests stays. Not a good look.
- grumpy long bearded star member in the van that wouldn’t acknowledge us.

Overall a pleasant stay, but due to a few issues as described above I’d rank it 3 stars out of 5.

Located a short walking distance from the Bright CBD, leave the car at the park. Cabins are clean and tidy, well appointed, and the newer 2 story cabins are great. The Sky Lounge is great for large groups.  The coffee shop provides good coffee, and light food. This is cycling heaven.

One of the best places I have ever stayed. The staff is really nice and friendly. The place is clean and beautiful. Lot of cabin options to choose from with couple of different views and add ons. Definitely recommend the place as I have stayed here months for work.

In good walking distance to the town. Clean and friendly.
The internet kept dropping out.
Powered camp-site was perfect, along with the all the kitchen, bathroom and entertainment facilities. Also just a short walk to the river and town centre 👍
Clean, comfortable, great location. What else can you ask for.

We stayed in the buckland (tree top villa) and the photos were a bit misleading. The cabin is located right at the edge of caravan park next to the road and parking lot. Lots of ants when we got in that thankfully got taken care of by bug spray left in the cabin which suggests it's a common problem. I expected much more for the price they charge. Fantastic location though - 5 min walk down to the river. I'd stay again but definitely not in that cabin.

Comfortable cabins. Very good position just beside water park. Not far away from restaurants and super market. Reception staff very kind and helpful. Only problem is the noise during evening as the live show will last long to deep night over 9.30pm, which is not friendly to the family close to centre area, especially family with seniors and children.
It's a okay park but no pool which is a con.....but its just near the Ovens river so also a pros

Bright in my opinion is the most beautiful place in Australia and then stay in BIG 4 holiday Park makes it 10× better because it's in the middle of the town. Plus the park its self has so many amenities for the guest .  The staff  is wonderful and welcoming. Would definitely recommend 11/10.

Booked for 3 weeks. Park looked lovely and peaceful.
Leafy and very nice.
First 9 days had 6 feral kids on bicycles speeding around the park in front of our van and doing jumps and skids, nearly hit our van and car and my elderly mum. So put a rock there at our site they jumped that, do put the caravan park tables and chairs there that stopped them. One child was 4yrs old with a dummy on an electric bike going around and around till 9 :15 pm for the first 9 days. Guests either side were complaining as well.
Now comes the coffee van .do yourself a favour and stand in ensuite site 44 when coffee van is open and listen to the THUMP THUMP THUMP THUMP that vibrates through our van of the idiot making coffee and emptying the coffee grinds. Then on sat last week we couldn't hear our TV for the screaming and running and adults in the sky lounge with dogs up there barking and running about.
Then wednesday morning we were awoken by some deashit in front of our caravan abusing a food delivery truck driver  with foul language my elderly  mum didn't need to hear about driving  too fast as his partners kid was killed in a van Park by a driver going too fast.  Probably  irresponsible  parenting like these idiots.
Oh then the next week with the dog behind us running though our site and knowing over our washing.
Barking Dogs.
Then came the basket ball bouncing in our van site.
Then the cricket match with balls hitting our van and car.
So people be warned...if you want a nice relaxing van Park don't come here We will never be back
Ever. Oh forgot about the power cuts and water cuts and nail guns and drilling for new cabins.
Yes thanks for reply but nobody abides by your rules re bikes after dark helmets swearing smoking ir barking dogs. Uou need to do a check nightly.

Had such a positive experience staying here this weekend! Lovely staff and wonderful facilities. Such a stunning caravan park, and perfectly located (just a short walk from the river and town). Thanks for having us. We cn't wait to come back!
Great deals, great accommodation, excellent staff and service! Thank you to the whole team!

We stayed in Buckland cabin. It was very comfortable, clean and well equiped. The Park is well located to the centre of Bright and lovely walks. We were very impressed with the facilities and will definitely go back with our van.

This park is the best we've stayed in over the years with facilities, trees, location to town & just in general, 10 out of 10.

Two things I didn't  like was the credit card charge of $7 to 8.00 which I'm guessing I've paid twice with deposit online, it's a bit rude really. I'll be paying cash or direct next time.

Next is the threat of a $500 penalty fee if you don't put the rubbish out in bigger bins. When I'm paying nearly $400 a night, I don't expect to be expected to put the rubbish out.
Heated amenities, great location and a short walk to town.
we stayed in a bungalo .  it was clean warm and a great price. walk to the bright brewery ...    🍺

One should visit this place... It's nice and located near to small town with all the things which is necessary to go on.
In my opinion it's a best location to stay if you need to visit Mount Hotham and Falls Creek for snow.
Everything was perfect. No complaints at all.

If you are travelling to Mt Hotham or Falls Creek this place is the best of the best.
Clean and tidy and always fresh linen and towels. Feels like home and bed omg bed are the most comfy.
Great location. Walk into main street.
There are way better caravan parks for kids. There was a tiny playground and that was about it. If I was a couple I would book here, if you have kids I would book somewhere else

Absolutely amazing experience living in the Bluff cabin in the Big4 Tasman Holiday park during our stay from 23-25th June,2023.

1)Rooms: Amazing rooms. Clean interior, fresh towels and all the dishes/equipment was kept sophisticatedly and cleanly in the room. There was a l master bed and 2 bunk beds so could accomodate 6 people. The bathroom and toilets were clean and all amenities provided. They had a good set up and gave us a good cabin feel.

2)Ambience: AMAZING ambience with lighting’s all around and a van which gives great coffee in the mornings and also pizzas in the night(not for free but worth it knowing you can walk up to a cafe and have a good time). The walk around the holiday park is also great. It’s a cute nice holiday park.

3)Location: 5 mins WALK to Bright town, so absolutely prime location.

4)Customer service: Bev and team were great and super helpful. Kind and polite. They offered to rent out chains as well and gave us good ideas regarding where to go during our time there. They even kept our rooms heated up since we were ariving post business hours

Small recommendation, if they could upgrade their TVs to smart TVs(not necessarily expensive ones) so that we can host netflix during a cold cold night in our warm beddings.(just a small suggestion)

In my opinion, 10/10 recommend. Don’t think to stay anywhere else if you’re in bright :)
Amazing ace, servoveand facilities
Perfect location

Cabin 15 was so cosy and warm! We had clear nights full of stars which means the nights get very chilly. But our cabin was toasty. The view from the lounge is gorgeous, as the sun sets behind Mt Buffalo. Then in the morning, it was beautiful watching the fog lift on the mountains. |Plenty of room for bikes on the deck for those doing the rail trails.|This park is on the Mt Buffalo Road, so nice and quiet after hours. A great location to stay while exploring the area.

We had a lovely time camping on the river. Facilities were clean and the showers were hot!
A special shout out to the manager, Dee. She went above and beyond to accommodate us with date changes, made such a fuss over our dog and made the kids feel super special! It was our second time here and we'll be back again!

MUST READ REVIEW!
Don’t waste your money staying here at all.
I’d give this place 0 stars if I could.
The lady (Dee) at the front is so rude and dismissive. We requested to check in an hour early so my son could have a nap after a long and exhausting drive and she refused, saying the cabin was still being cleaned. When we checked in an hour later the cabin wasn’t even clean. What did we wait for then?? The floors were so filthy, there was blood on the door, mould in the kitchen cupboard, and dust everywhere. When I complained on booking.com they received it and sent a staff member to confront us about our review saying we were lying so we showed all the photos to her. Dee then said she’d give a partial refund for it but couldn’t do it while we were there and once we left she had changed her mind and once again lied to us.
Will be going to Consumer Affairs Victoria.

We almost got misled by the name when booking. Discovery Parks – Mount Buffalo is actually located in Porepunkah, less than a 10 minute drive from Bright.

We stayed at a riverside powered site, and it was especially beautiful in autumn with the colours along the river.

The only downside was that the walk to the kitchen facilities was quite long from our site.

It is a great option when visiting Bright, and much more affordable than staying in town. I suspect many people may overlook this place because of the name.
Very nice park with river views

Thanks Dee and the team. This was our first stay but won’t be our last. Our campsite beside the river was magical,  the facilities were clean and modern and the location was perfect! Can’t wait to see you again 😊

We have stayed at this park 2 years in a row for the Easter long weekend. It is fantastic. Amazing location and super friendly staff especially Dee! Love the kids activities and visit from the Easter bunny.
Staff a bit abrasive at check in and amenities not  clean - even when toilets were cleaned the shower block was not.

Enjoyed a great stay! Park was amazing, kids enjoyed the river, close to lots of walks and activities in the area. Park manager Dee was super helpful accommodating our requests where possible and made our stay very enjoyable. Highly recommend

Such a great spot to stay. We managed to nab a site above the river. Kids were in there everyday.
Dee was really easy to deal with and accomodated our requests where possible.
We have a great cabin nice and clean and great location..

$75/night for an unpowered site where the grass hadn't been mowed for quite a while was a bit rich, even for a long weekend. On the upside the brand new mini amenities block adjacent to the site was clean and well laid out

Spent a couple of nights at Discovery Parks - Mount Buffalo. It's really well located for exploring the best parts of the Alpine Region.||Staff were very friendly, after hours check in was a breeze. Good facilities.||Our cabin was huge and in good condition. Car park directly outside. Kookaburras in the trees in the morning. Everything was great.

Always love coming back to Mt Buffalo Discovery Parks. Perfect location, great facilities and extremely helpful employees. Dee (Park Manager) went over and above when we had to make last minute changes to our booking. Wouldn’t have made it away on our holiday without her. Thank you! See you next year.

Mount Buffalo Porepunkah caravan park is a beautiful family park that I love staying at. Leaving for home today, just wanted to say thank you to the cleaning staff for the time put into cleaning the amenities. From the exhaust fans, tiles and grout it was a pleasure seeing the nice, clean, welcoming shower as some parks don’t take pride In their amenities.

We stayed on a powered site by the river, which was a great location. The kids absolutely loved tubing down the river -  definitely a highlight of the stay.

Facilities were okay overall, though there didn’t seem to be enough toilets for how busy the park was. It was manageable, but at peak times you could feel it.

Unfortunately, there was quite a bit of late-night partying which took away from the experience, especially for families. We also had an issue with a beagle like dog from a permanent site being allowed to roam freely and leave mess on other people’s sites, which wasn’t great and let the park down a bit. I spoke to my neighbours about it and they said it was common from that particular site and others had complained - so seems unfortunately plausible that despite multiple complaints nothing has been done.

Overall, a nice spot with lots of potential, especially for families, but some better enforcement of park rules and amenities would really improve the experience.
Powered site overlooking the Ovens River was delightful. Awesome support and service from Dee in the office. Two brilliant weeks of total relaxation.

We thought we were booking for the 'other' Mount Buffalo caravan park, further down the road. We were disappointed at first, but the park is in a great location, close to porepunkah town and the staff were lovely.

Where we were in the park had only access to one toilet and shower, which was not enough for all the people in that area, so be mindful when booking if you have accessibility issues and would prefer not to walk.

The park is right on the river and was accommodating for for dogs, which we really appreciated.

A few people hogged the river access by setting up there cabanas and leaving them for the week in the river, but just ask the kind staff where the other access is, as it's a little tricky to find, but worth the adventure.

The park is such a tranquil place. The entertainment organised by management on New Year’s Day was a real highlight. Perfect time in the afternoon and it felt like a serene gathering of people enjoying themselves. Bravo Discovery. Thank you to Dee the park manager for organising.

We booked a cabin, nice and clean accommodation. I had the most friendliest welcome from Dee, she was very helpful and gave up awesome advice on local activities and sights to see. Highly recommend the river on a hot day. Definitely booking my next holiday here!

First time staying in the park. It is a big park but doesn't feel big. It is nice and quiet, kids roving around having fun. The river side camping is magnificent 👌
We will definitely be coming back

Our stay at Discovery Park Porpunkah was disappointing due to the behaviour of the receptionist, Dee. She was extremely rude, dismissive, and unhelpful from the moment we arrived. Basic questions were met with attitude, and there was no attempt at customer service or courtesy. It really took away from what should have been a relaxing stay. A friendlier and more professional approach at reception would make a huge difference. I have traveled extensively around Australia for many years and have never encountered a receptionist who could possibly be more rude than Dee. If it weren’t for such a lovely caravan park and location we would never think to come back.

Well presented and very clean , Dee and the staff all friendly and helpful.
Would highly reccomend.

We just spent 3 night at the Discovery Mount Buffalo caravan park. We had the best time, great location, lots to do in the local area. Short drive to Bright, which is absolutely beautiful!! We were lucky enough to be there when the market was on.

The staff at the caravan park (Dee, and Vic) were so friendly and accomodating!! They went out if there way to ensure we had an enjoyable stay with the kids. Thank you!! We will be back!! X
Stayed there from the 12/09 until the 15/09 and we had three cabins all clean and well maintained,Dee was very helpful,would highly recommend.
Good stay, only negative was sink in bathroom was slow to drain.

Dee has the most amazing and caring service I’ve ever come across. It just made my holiday. Just Wow!!!
Thank you Dee.

Friendly staff are always keen to improve guests experience. Well equipped cabins and park, easy access and very well maintained public facilities. Tip for all future guests, reach out to Dee on the phone instead of booking via other portals. She will look after you all :)

Our first time staying here and very impressed with how clean and bright the cabin is. From the bedrooms with electric blanket to the amenities in kitchen and bathroom. Very modern and spotless. Beautiful view is so easy on the eyes especially The Ovens River. We were greeted by Dee at the reception and she was so friendly and helpful. Thank you for being so accommodating, Dee. We truly enjoyed our stay.
Cute little spot for winter camping - fire pit, riverside and close to town 🤎
Lovely lady at the reception.
Very good place to stay overnight

A good park if your plan is to hit the snow at Buffalo. Playground ok. No indoor hangout(games room etc)  If you are booking a riverside caravan and you have a large set up. Ensure you make this clear as some of the sites aren't really suitable. I can't see a dodge ram with a 24 footer being able to back up and we are just a 4wd with a 16 foot expanda. Would be hard if they were fully booked but we were lucky and were able to just switch sites because it wasn't busy.

Facilities getting closer to a revamp but still functional.
We were on the riverside power sites,great views . The staff are super friendly and the amenities are great we had the best stay ,and you can have a fire by the river ,thanks
Great location to stay.
What a beautiful park the staff are there to please are polite ,professional ,and happy the amenities are the best in 20,000 ks. Great location central to everything.
Excellent site and great park location. Toilet block was warm and clean. Can walk everywhere. Loved it.

We loved the facilities, the cleanliness is second to none & the staff are lovely. Cant explain how much I loved the warm, fresh & so, so clean bathrooms!!
Just a beautiful park, see you next time!! Mel & Dave
Nice park right in town. Good facilities.
Didn't go there! Can't review this place!
Perfect for the motorhome
Just arrived here in love with this place already
Great park, great location! Not sure if you can get any more central than the Bright Riverside Holiday Park!

We had a fantastic stay at Bright Riverside Holiday Park! The location is perfect—right by the river and surrounded by beautiful scenery. It’s a peaceful and relaxing place, ideal for a getaway.

The accommodation was clean, modern, and well-equipped with everything we needed. The park facilities were also well maintained, and the overall atmosphere was very welcoming.

Perfect for families or anyone looking to unwind in nature. W

Excellent location and clean accommodation. |The riverside cabin was not luxurious and showed significant signs of wear and tear. Therefore felt expensive for accommodations which was very basic and in need of updating to justify the nightly charge of over five hundred dollars.
Great location on the river.  5 minute walk into town.  Spotless amenities
Such a welcoming experience staying in the alpine pods with so much to do and the service was ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ thankyou to Nicol for making our country escape one to remember
Truly a hidden gem , cosy, beautiful rooms and very stylish. I will come back for another visit to enjoy spring in Bright thank you for the amazing stay Nicol

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Beautiful Riverside Stay in Porepunkah

We recently stayed at a lovely caravan park right on the Ovens River in Porepunkah and absolutely loved it. The setting is stunning—peaceful, surrounded by trees, and just steps from the water. Perfect for a morning swim or sitting by the river with a coffee.

The park itself is clean, quiet, and well looked after. The sites were spacious and shady, and the amenities were spotless. It’s also pet-friendly, which was a big bonus for us!

We especially enjoyed the bike path into Bright and being so close to wineries and Mount Buffalo. Whether you’re relaxing by the river or out exploring, it’s the ideal base in the Alpine region.

Highly recommend for couples, families, or anyone wanting a peaceful nature escape!
Fantastic location and clean cabin
Perfect location to spend weekend with family with easy booking here, especially snow time. Dee helped us regarding our query and other staff was lovely too.
very nice location, would come back again - everything clean, Dee was great! I had to borrow an extension cord and she was so helpful!

We booked two powered sites over the Anzac weekend. These were river fronts.., however we were given two sites on different levels. The sites are very small and a tight fit., and you butt up against your Neighbour, not very private if you  relaxing. It would seem the place is a dog park, and the barking is annoying.
Now the good things, the field staff are great and really help you out.
The shower and toilet facilities are very clean and modern.  It’s only a short walk to town .

It was the best experience!
Amazing management who genuinely care about their guests — they went above and beyond to make our stay perfect. Definitely going back. Thank you so much!

The cabins we were staying was very clean, comfortable, and very homey. They also turned on the heater before we arrived which was really a great thing since it was a cold day during our visit.

Bathroom and toilet were very clean. The kitchen was equipped enough for us. The bedrooms were provided with electric blankets.

The entire property was really great with access to Ovens River and was really gorgeous.
Great service & beautiful camping along the river. Close to everything. Will come back.

Please be mindful of the potential discriminatory behaviour when dealing with reception at Discovery Parks – Mount Buffalo. I recently called the park to enquire about my booking. After asking for my name, the receptionist immediately assumed I had booked through an overseas agency, saying, “When you book through those overseas companies, they never send us all the information.”

I can’t be sure if this assumption was based on my name or my accent — but it felt that way. When I clarified that I had booked directly through G’day.com, the receptionist replied, “Oh sorry, you booked through G’day.com.”

Throughout the call, the receptionist’s attitude was rude and unprofessional. I understand people can be short-staffed or stressed, but that should never be used to excuse discriminatory or condescending behaviour. Both my friends and I were left feeling deeply uncomfortable by this experience.

This review will remain public until Discovery Parks provide a proper explanation or apology. If no action is taken — or if this comment is removed — I will escalate the matter through other platforms and appropriate channels. This is about accountability and making sure no one else has to go through the same.

This place is incredible.

From the warm welcome, I believe the lady was called Dee, the amenities and the location - perfect for walks into the forest and into Punkah.

Traveled with two kids, a 4 and a 2 year old who loved the park and the walks.

Just spent 5 nights here. Great park with a site on the river.   Very quiet during autumn 5 minutes to Bright 15 minutes to Myrtleford. Amenities look like they have been updated recently and very clean, having a fire was great.
Manager Dee was great. Contacted us when husband left phone behind and dropped off to us. Read reviews regarding Dee but we had no problems at all totally opposite.
Will definitely be back.

First the goods things… great field staff, very helpful. The amenities block was spotless , well maintained and ample facilities. We booked  two powered sites… the river was great. Now the nir so good… the two sites were in different levels and extremely tight. As for the river front sites we were packed in like sardines. No privacy at all… it was a perfect dog pound .. continual barking …. So not at all relaxing as one would expect from the booking web site happy snaps. And yes it was a long weekend…
Great location over the bridge - well run. Not comfortable will get water discharged into the Ovens river by the riverside vans (it’s 2025)

We had an amazing experience camping at Mt Buffalo Discovery Park, such a beautiful special place to visit anytime of the year. The park grounds are always neat and tidy it’s a credit to the staff.
Thank you Dee and the team for making our stay so warm and welcoming will definitely be coming back again.

Not very welcoming at all. Was a bit late getting in due to traffic and young children requiring many stops on the way in due to a very long drive but still arrived when reception was open, however not a warm reception. Stayed in a superior cabin. Very outdated, the shower was terrible, running from hot to cold continually and very hard to control the temperature. Uncomfortable pillows to sleep on. Some of the blinds do not open/close at all. Playground isn’t suitable for younger children. Very expensive for what was offered. Wouldn’t stay here again unfortunately.

I couldn’t have asked for a better 3 days, cabin was great clean everything you need.
Dee was very helpful and accommodating, thanks again Dee will be back 😊😊

Recently, my husband and I decided on a last minute long weekend away and picked an area we've never been to. I can't recommend this place strongly enough but if you want a big bustling loud and shiny caravan park, then this isn't for you. This is a quaint, back to nature caravan park, where the distant mountains and river are the attraction. It's so well treed, adding to the atmosphere particularly in autumn but offering lovely shade in the summer. The river is tranquil but I absolutely loved watching the children float by on inflatable rafts down the river past our camp site. The lush green grass to the river's edge. There is even a pump track for the big kids at heart who still think they can ride a bike.
We booked a powered river site and was close to the wonderful, spacious and clean amenities. I loved the showers. So spacious and perfect water pressure and temperature. The communal kitchen was brilliant. Almost as equipped as mine at home.
We were able to ride our bikes to Bright and back. Absolutely stunning. Enjoyed a stroll to the local pub for dinner one night and even a dip in the pool and river.
We loved exploring the surrounding area, particularly Mt Buffalo and Beechworth. We did many walks to see waterfalls, gorges and peak tops.
We loved the place so much, we extended our trip and stayed the week. The place has so much to offer and the caravan park is the perfect base to stay at.

We had a fantastic stay at Discovery Park Mount Buffalo last weekend. The park offers spacious campsites, making it easy to set up and enjoy a comfortable experience without feeling crowded. The bathrooms were impressively clean and well-maintained through our visit, which made a significant difference.

The kitchen area was spacious, well-equipped, and always tidy and organised. It even had soap, sponges, and clean tea towels available!

From the moment we made our booking, the staff was accommodating, arranging our spot next to our friends, which we really appreciated. This made our experience even better!

The only small improvement we would suggest is providing dog waste bags in the park, as this would be a convenient addition for dog owners. Otherwise, everything was excellent, and we would definitely return and recommend this park to others!

My kids and I decided to book a site last weekend by the river. If you’re not a dog person, find another camping spot, as dogs seems to rule the roost and bark continuously throughout the day and night here. We came later in the day when no one was manning the reception so we collected our map for our campsite.
Maybe I could have invested more time in looking for our spot but as it was growing dark and on the map visually it stated we were on the river (directly opposite a permanent cabin/holiday home right next to the recreation reserve) so we set up on a grassed comfortable area (which was apparently a recreation area packed with people NOT) that looked like how the map was set out as I couldn’t see the site number (there was no direction signage at the end of the park entrance road). The next morning we received a call while in Bright informing us we were in the incorrect spot and to move, and informing me in a patronising way that all sites were clearly signed. We did see the signage for the site but not until dark when we plugged our fridge in. The site ended up being 2.5 -3 metres across from the offending/incorrect area, so I moved it, dragged the tent across to a hard bald piece of surface, and in the process ripped out a part of our tent. The surface was undeniably rock and clay and was a mission to re hammer pegs into. In the end our tent was lopsided being broken and was extremely uncomfortable for the second night. For one parent and three kids, this was too much for some kind of relaxing get away. More signage is definitely needed for directions at each end of the streets in the park, especially for those whom have never been to the park or when it is dark. The people with dogs in this facility definitely have some sort of priority and don’t seem to have any kind of consideration about their dogs barking at all times of the day and evening. We will not be paying $80 per night for a powered campsite which is on a bald piece of rock next to barking dogs. Other than all of that, the ducks, river and other wildlife were perfect. Next time we will be seeking free camping or somewhere else who have consideration for all campers as I love Porepunkah, it is a magic part of the earth.

I recently stayed in one of the pet friendly cabins with my puppy and it was absolutely perfect. Accomodation was clean, comfortable and well equipped. There was heaps of space for me to walk my dog, he especially loved playing in the creek. The whole grounds were incredibly clean and well maintained. I’ll definitely be back soon 😊
Fantastic location. Horrible manager Dee. There are other parks in the area that are much nicer and less expensive with friendlier service.

Dee (manager) is one of the most wicked people I've ever met and lies through her teeth. She is cold as ice & picks and chooses when she wants to be accommodating. Location is amazing thanks to nature.  Caravan sites have nothing to offer unless you are river front. Most sites are rock, dust or hay & about 200mtrs from water (no water views). Pretty steep for $109 a night. Amenities are clean but showers are quite a distance away from sites. Mel (receptionist) is lovely when "dicky knee" (Dee) isn't in the background undermining her. Great location. Short walk into small but lovely little town.

A fabulous stay at Discovery Parks - Mount Buffalo. Dee is a wonderful host, and her personal touch added for an extra special visit. The park is excellent with a pool, river, playground and excellent amenities, and only a 5 minute walk from town. We will absolutely be back - we've already booked our next stay. 5 stars all round!

Very disappointing. The sites are too small for modern large caravans. The grounds are dusty with very patchy grass and many loose and sizable stones / rocks on the surface. The sites had not been mowed and there were many weeds up to 40 cm. Even the small grassed area in the swimming pool was in very poor condition. Amenities are insufficient a  queues to the toilets became the norm as the long weekend got underway. Sorry, but at almost $80 per night we expect more and will not be returning.

We are very thankful with the help we received from the management here at this park, they went out of their way to give us a great site for the size of our van and with needing to stay an extra night while waiting for some mail to turn.
Would definitely recommend this park to stay at.

What a joke the sites are tiny and cramped tight with vans, should have stayed to free camping. Amenities are filthy every time we have used them, We have had to clean them first.
80% of the park is old permanent vans.
Small domestic size pool. The only thing this park has going for it is that it's on the river.

Impressive- Clean as a Whistle!

I don’t think I’ve come across a better camp kitchen or toilet / shower facilities than here. Immaculate.

The Manager, Dee, is effervescent in her welcome and went out of her way to assist. Fabulous stay.
Lovely quiet (when we visited) park on the Ovens river. close to interesting places like Bright and Beechworth. We had a very relaxing stay.
What a gem of a park. Just 5 minutes from Bright. Beautiful sites with the Ovens River flowing 2 steps behind your campsite. Park managers lovely and helpful. Will definitely be back.

We went with a group. Nice campsites along the river with shade. All amenities available for RVs. No shop but 2min drive to Porepunkah. Very good large air conditioned camp kitchen and BBQ.
Only thing to spoil it is the proliferation of old and decrepit on-site vans. They look privately owned but maybe only used during the snow season as we saw no-one.

We recently spent 6 nights at Mt Buffalo park in Porepunkah but have been holidaying there every year with our dog Bobby.
Dee, the manager has been wonderful, as always very helpful, friendly and accommodating. Pet friendly cabins are perfect for us, clean and well equipped. The facilities at this park are very good and well maintained. The location is great, walking distance to the Porepunkah pub and cafes.  Thank you Dee for a wonderful stay,  we look forward to coming back soon.

We recently spent 5 nights in our van at this lovely Porepunkah tourist park on the banks of the Ovens River. Dee (manager) was super helpful, friendly and accommodating when we asked to change the site assigned to us and we enjoyed the sight and sound of the river bubbling along next to our site. The facilities at this park were top notch and the caravan sites are a generous size. There are lots of trees for shade and beautiful grassed areas alongside the river to picnic or relax on. Thanks for ensuring a wonderful stay Dee, we look forward to coming back!

We had stayed there for two nights and it was really hot and the fan was broken it was loud and ratily also the top bunk beds railing was almost about to fall off its hinges. It was very unsafe but overall it was ok
Amazing stay! Can’t wait to come back. 😊
We have left the gravel pit in the park. The price charged they could supply shade cloth- or astro turf as a slab. Sites too small for 20' plus foot Vans. Won't be back.

The location is the best thing about this place. However, the accommodation itself leaves a lot to be desired. I am in Alpine Pod 15. The room is clean, but very hot and the aircon did not do anything. Put aircon on at 22 as told by reception, but it was not effective at all and it is hotter inside than out. Tried to go out for reprieve, but then the code I was given didn't open the boomgate, so had to call for help. Thankfully, someone answered quickly. And was able to go. Upon return, room is still stifling hot. Cannot sleep at all and want to return to Melbourne, but concerned about wildlife so waiting til first light. If I were to return to Bright again, it would probably be at a different accommodation provider or it would be in Autumn or Winter if I were to stay here again.

Sent to campsites which already had campers and the dump point was not working and disgustingly full. Too expensive for what you get which was a dusty camp-site a long way from the amenities. Nearness to the river was a positive.
Perfect location to walk into town. Plenty of bike path options so definitely take your push bike. Beautiful and scenic to sit and relax down by the river.
Had a fantastic time with my family will be coming back again 😊

We had a powered site for our camper trailer and our parents stayed in the Alpine Pod. Both were excellent. The sites were large, concrete and level with easy access. The camp kitchen was amazing with everything you need and a lovely BBQ area. The cabin was cute, cosy and clean. Fantastic location on the river and walk to town. Would stay again.

I experienced very poor customer service over the phone , person who attended the call in the morning mentioned different price then the time of the booking, despite confirming  the price with her 3 times. The difference was around 200 $ per day , there is no mention on website that it will be additional charge for more than 2 person as 2 bedroom riverside villa is for 6 people. She  said she can do the same price anytime if I want to confirm the dates with someone, I literally called after 2 hours and she declined to do the same price. Atleast don’t lie whatever you said and value your customer.

Great location, rude receptionist, very tight sites. I booked 6 powered sites, they put me on the dryest rocky ground even though I told them I'm staying in a tent. 3 toilets for the whole caravan Park not enough. Showers get flooded mind you its new. Hardly any grass. Unsafe 2 plumbing points next to the amenities and the clothesline.
High prices.
Will never be back, there are other places as nice but cheaper and very friendly staff with much better service.

We are staying here now on a powered site, this place is impeccably clean and well maintained, all services we need plus so much more, tv entertainment area, kitchen is so clean and well presented with so many appropriate facilities, the whole layout and the positioning near the river is perfect. Staff have been very friendly and the cabins/villas look so good we want to come back and stay in one. Bathroom facilities are the cleanest and most well maintained I’ve ever seen. It’s honestly so well maintained the whole place. My partner and I are very impressed

Very disappointed, as I booked a cabin but due to emergency illness I couldn't go, asked for credit at least they refused even tho I mentioned i will provide a letter from emergency department but there was no understanding i paid almost $1000 and my kids were supper excited but the policy made me so sad

Fantastic location, quiet and peaceful, the dog friendly cabins are clean and appointed well. The receptionist ( Mel ) was great, friendly, helpful and knowledgeable. Ready to accommodate every request.

Stayed here to ride the bike trails at Bright.after a day here we booked an extra 2 days as we loved it so much .Lovely leafy green grounds with king parrots in the trees above us and the river bubbling away just along the side of the site.

We booked 2 dog friendly cabins & had the most wonderful time. Dee was so lovely handling all our questions & changes to our bookings before we even arrived. The whole team at the park were super accommodating & friendly. The park has a lovely setting with views to Mt Buffalo on one side & river frontage on the other. We used the bbq near the pool which was immaculate & worked extremely well. The cabins were comfy & clean & had everything we needed. Also loved that the bike path is right out the front. Thanks Dee & all the staff for helping make our family weekend away very special & I would love to return.
Beautiful place to stay ,powered sites river front dog friendly loved our stay 4 nights stay

River front powered site was lovely but some are hard to get into. Amenities were clean. Liked the location. Good value. But there were too many crusty permanent vans there which kind of wrecked the vibe of the place.

We had such a wonderful time here. Dee was outstanding from start to finish. She was awesome every time we also came in with the kids to buy ice creams and treats. Such a peaceful wonderful place to stay! Thank you Dee and we will be back. See you soon.
Love it
Rooms are ok thanks for having us.
Lovely staff. Thanks to Dee for helping out a passing traveller!

Perfect for what we needed. Newly built modern cabin with a great kitchen. Aircon/heater worked a treat. Plenty of room for 2 people. Bed was comfy perfect for a day full of snowboarding.
The bathroom was small but again it was perfect for us. Would stay again and recommend, thank you

so good! such a nice and spacious place with anything you may need - oven, stovetop, fridge, dining table and much more. great value for money and excellent staff service even when checking in after hours. very much recommend for groups of 2-4 staying on a budget.

We loved our 1 night stay! Our after-hours check-in was super easy and the heater had been left on for us. The space was perfect for two of us and had everything we needed - and we were super close to Mt Buffalo and Bright.

Our delux cabin was a great size for our family of 5. 2 bedrooms and a spacious lounge, it was comfortable for the kids to lounge and watch TV. We had a late check in and the room had the heater on waiting for us. Fabulous view of the river, a lovely quite spot. Thanks Dee, we will definitely be back.

I recently stayed at this cabins with a group of friends, and we had a fantastic experience. The cabin itself was cozy, well-maintained, and provided all the amenities we needed for a comfortable stay. The location was perfect for a relaxing getaway, offering beautiful scenery and a peaceful atmosphere.

The service was excellent; the staff specially (DEE ) was friendly and responsive to our needs, ensuring that our stay was enjoyable. We especially appreciated their attention to detail and willingness to accommodate any special requests we had.

Overall, I highly recommend this cabin for anyone looking to enjoy a memorable stay with friends or family. We would definitely return for another visit!
Really friendly staff, clean cabins and have the give some activities for the car.

We arrived Friday after a long drive and we were looking forward to a quiet evening, but instead one of the permanent residents chose to have a party till way past midnight, laughter, talk ,music  and drinking despite it’s a 10 pm curfew.
If you like to camp on the top of each other, have dogs barking, kids screaming and no privacy, then this is the place.
We will not be coming back!!!!

We have not long returned from our few days at this wonderful park!
To be honest we were not expecting much given we were able to take our fur babies with us inside the cabin accommodation. Well.. we were very wrong! Absolutely spotless, fantastic comfortable bed, excellent linen,  and the wonderful staff had even turned our heater on.
The grounds staff work day and night keeping the park pristine and from our interactions with them you are left with the impression that they take pride in making the place wonderful.
River runs at the back of the park which is just spectacular. We had a wine, cheese from Milawa and watched our dogs frolic in the water. Lots of pet friendly walking trails and pubs!
The only down side is that we didn’t extend our stay. We will absolutely be back.
Shout out to Dee who was a pleasure to book with. You will not be disappointed staying here. Just fabulous. Vanessa and Mick.

Stayed a week in a Superior 2 bed cabin - Very clean rooms and linen - credit to the cleaners!
Squeaky bathroom doors and toilet flush button getting stuck.
Great location .
Highly recommended
Enjoyable couple of nights, modern clean and ample facilities. Grassy site overlooking the Buckland River.

Loved it for a 2 night getaway. Stayed in a deluxe cabin. We went to the area for an Italian festival in Myrtleford. Great location. 10 mins to Bright. 15 to 20 mins to Myrtleford. Amazing and beautiful in Autumn.

1 Star – Only for the Location and Scenery

We stayed as two families in the Riverside Villa and paid over $1,000 for two nights. Unfortunately, the experience with management was extremely disappointing.

Despite the villa clearly being designed for multiple guests, management refused to provide us with the access code for the shared toilets on-site, insisting that we use the single toilet inside the villa. Strange, but we accepted it and moved on.

Quiet hours begin at 10pm, yet the venues directly across the road are loud well past that time — again, not a major issue for us, but worth noting.

What was an issue was discovering that the price of the villa drops to nearly half if booked on arrival, meaning we ended up paying almost double simply for booking in advance.

The biggest frustration came at check-out. Even though we thoroughly cleaned the villa, removed all rubbish, washed the dishes that morning, and left everything in the drying rack, we were still charged an additional cleaning fee. When we questioned it, we were told the dishes needed to be “rewashed” and that a “dirty tablecloth” needed cleaning. The irony? The tablecloth was our own disposable one used for an anniversary cake — and after explaining that, the staff member still insisted it was “dirty” and needed to be thrown out (as disposable items usually are…).

We were also told the pan had “burn marks on the bottom.” It’s your pan, your gas stove — perhaps consider better cookware instead of penalising guests.

After checking Google reviews, it’s very clear there is a pattern of a certain racial group being targeted with these extra cleaning charges, which is extremely concerning.

Overall, we paid over $1,000 for two nights and were left feeling frustrated and unwelcome. The only redeeming quality of this stay was the beautiful river running through the property — Bright itself is stunning, and we still made the most of our trip despite the poor service.

I would not recommend staying here based on our experience.

Perfect family destination! The stream infront of our villa was so serene and fun for the whole family to enjoy. Lots of activities for the kids and wonderful serenity walks to go on. Cant wait to go again!!

Best location ever, loads of trees if you want to be by the river or one row back. People are beautiful and amazingly helpful. Facilities super modern and clean. Looks like there's some gorgeous new cabins around too so you absolutely can't go wrong
Thank you to Sheree, Anthony and the team our stay was awesome and they were very helpful.. Thank you!

First time staying here and I can't wait to come back. The facilities are all modern and clean. The place has amazing views of the mountains by the river and with in a very short walk to the town centre. Sheree and AJ were friendly and kind to deal with and nothing was a hassle. Thank you guys again.

Great place . best staff, they go above and beyond for service .
Thanks you guys for hospitality. Sorry bout the sort notice
Great place to stay. Central to town, staff are great and facilities are excellent.
Excellent campsite for the kids

Let us stay in a cabin at the very last minute even after reception closed because our tent was mouldy when we arrived. Was so kind and helpful. Amenities were also the cleanest I’ve ever seen in a holiday park. Had the best experience ever thanks to the staff and it was the most beautiful location!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Absolutely loved our stay at Bright Riverside Caravan Park! From the moment we arrived, the staff made us feel genuinely welcome — friendly, helpful, and always up for a chat that makes you feel like part of the family. The grounds are immaculate — lush, green, and perfectly maintained — you can tell there’s real pride in the place.

The location couldn’t be better: right by the river and only a short stroll into town, close to everything yet peaceful enough to feel like your own slice of nature. Whether you’re after relaxation or adventure, this park nails it.

Couldn’t fault a thing — clean amenities, beautiful setting, and a team that clearly cares about their guests. Five stars all the way — we’ll definitely be back!
Amazing location... the sound of the Ovens River is the cherry on top... very tranquil.

The Bright Riverside Holiday Park, is a great place to stay to explore the local area. The location is 10/10 on the Ovens River and a couple hundred meters walk into town! The cabin we stayed in was a little dated but clean and facing the river so all good! They also have new cabins if you prefer! The fire pits are not reserved and you can order wood from reception. Definitely recommend if you’re visiting Bright!

We really enjoyed our stay here. The location was amazing, within easy walking distance to the shops. The amenities were excellent, with showers and toilets always spotless. Everything we needed for a comfortable and convenient stay was right there. Highly recommend, will definitely be back!

BEWARE OF THIS PARKS DYNAMIC PRICING POLICY.
If you book online in advance during the off season, you could pay in excess of $100/night more than if you booked just prior to arrival.
If I had booked online I would have paid $360/night for a cabin that was being charged at $240/night when we arrived and the park was practically empty.
This dynamic pricing policy is not designed to offer a discount to fill vacancies, instead it is designed to extort as much money as possible from unsuspecting customers, and it's a policy that management is proud of. BE WARNED.

We recently returned from x 3 families sharing 3 beautiful cabins in Bright Riverside Holiday Park in Bright. This was our second stay and both times it was delightful! This must be the best managed and maintained park in Victoria. The setting is glorious with the Ovens river on their perimeter along with ducks and very sweet ducklings. |The grounds are stunning so clean and well maintained, and the cabins equally brilliant. Really we cannot recommend every  aspect (especially the reception folk) enough. Nothing was any trouble. We will return!

Riverside Park is an absolute gem! From the moment we arrived, everything exceeded our expectations. The facilities are second to none – the camp kitchen is the best we’ve seen, beautifully set up with a spacious outdoor area that makes cooking and socialising a pleasure. The bathrooms are modern, spotless, and thoughtfully heated in winter, which made our stay extra comfortable.

The staff were warm, welcoming, and always ready to help, adding a personal touch that made us feel right at home. The location is simply perfect – right on the river with stunning views and just a short stroll into town.

This park really does have it all: fantastic amenities, a beautiful setting, and wonderful people. We can’t recommend it highly enough and can’t wait to return!
Stayed in onsite cabin. Week away Mountain bike riding with friend. Great stay and very nice area to explore.

Excellent park.  Originally booked 2 night stay on a Powered site but ended up extending a further 2 nights.  Easy to access site on the river very picturesque.  Amenities were very clean and close to all sites.  Friendly and helpful staff and close to Bright

3vans  attended our stay  was cut short  by 1day out of 8  due to a specialist appointment brought forward ,and we were very disappointed to hear they don’t give credits.it’s not the money but the attitude which upset us  .The ground staff and front office  were good
But Big 4is looking good Max

We recently stayed on a powered river view site over Anzac weekend and absolutely loved it!

So great for our little kids with the grassy recreation area and crystal clear shallow water to splash around in.

We had beautiful weather, dry, cold nights and sunny days. Loved sitting by the fire pit each night with friends and riding into bright for a play at the playground and a bite to eat and drink at the bright brewery.

Only thing is there is no family bathroom, so it was very challenging to bath our kids in the trough in women’s bathroom. The plug didn’t fit and the water temperature tap was almost impossible to operate!

Apart from that we highly recommend and will be back next year! Thankyou :)
Great place. Always kept clean. Close to shops and pub.
Fantastic site perfectionally run. See you next time. We'll be back.
Beautiful park and great riverside camp ground. Quiet and well maintained, good facilities.
Beautiful Place. Great facilities.

We spend a lot of time in this beautiful park and always love our stay!
Our recent stay over Easter had a rocky start with campers next to us (see the below 1 star review mentioning cutting their Easter trip short) Their dogs barked all night and were very reactive and uncontrolled on long leads - running at kids, dogs and anyone walking past their site. Thankfully they had muzzles on. Discovery parks management handled it very well following reports from very concerned campers, as safety was an issue.
Unfortunately the owners seem to be completely ignorant to what really went on (once again, see the below 1 star review) Thanks again to park management!
We highly recommend this park for its river frontage and location to porepunkahs walking trails, shops and Pub!

Had booked for the Easter long weekend with our dogs. We had stayed one night. Our dogs had stayed on their leads the entire time. They barked occasionally when the ocasional off lead dog ran into our camp site or the spotted the occasional possum at night. In general they were pretty quiet.

Campers next to us had a young baby and the lady came to our camp site while I was away having a shower telling us we have to go. We make too much noise.

When the park manager came to say we had an official complaint about our dogs my anxiety as to high to stay next to other campers that clearly had zero empathy all tolerance to camp next to us. We called out Easter holiday short. Won’t be going back.

We stayed in a Deluxe 2BR cabin.|Cabin was well appointed. Linen was crisp and spotless. Bathroom was spotless. Means that you have to keep washing up after each meal, which is not what you want to do when on holiday.|Biggest downside of the park is that there are so many permanents, and their places are poorly kept and dirty. And you have to go past them to your cabin. |Service was efficient but a bit gruff.

I stayed for 4 nights. Rooms in the cabin were ok. But the customer service of the receptionist was terrible over the phone and in person. Her reply was we can’t check in early and even we can’t be in the park until the check in time. The receptionist has asked my wife to leave the park and come back at the check in time.
