Real nice tidy clean and friendly good place

Site was nice and grassy, however i think a little more work needs to go into the unpowered site, especially more trees, there isn't shade whatsoever. Would be nice to have a proper amenities block closer to the unpowered sites as well, rather than just toilets.

In saying that the main amenities block was a great size and well maintained, the little demountable toilets near the unpowered sites were also in great condition. Pool was also bloody awesome
Best getaway if you are going to Bright and surrounding areas.

We had a great stay, the cabins and facilities were beautiful and clean and the service by park management was fantastic, they also went above and beyond to help me with a booking issue (which was my fault not theirs).

We were pleasantly surprised to find the park backed on to a gorgeous large creek with calm water that was perfect for the kids and pup to play for hours. With only a short drive to Bright and myrtleford and short walk to the town centre. We will definitely be back!

We camped here over the Australia Day long weekend. It was our first time in the area and found it was in the perfect location for Mount Buffalo, Bright and Beechworth. Upon arrival we had people in our spot who weren’t supposed to be, but the staff were more than helpful and gave us a choice of campsites. We ended up in a perfect site on the water, with plenty of space. We will stay here again!
Ice
Accommodation and staff were lovely. Location is perfect, really short walk to town. place is kept spotless!

Amazing spot to stay in our caravan on the way to the snow. It was a bit strange to be camping in front of a luxury cabin, but we had a great view of the river. Great facilities and short walk to everything.

Terrific holiday park close to the centre of Bright and right on the Ovens river. We stayed in the Superior Villas and will be coming back. Clean, bright, modern, lovely bathroom and well appointed. Perfect for a family of four with kids sharing a room
Last month visited there with family & friends. They charged us extra money for cleaning and the staff there was very rude. Not happy with the service.

Went with 5 mates. The cabins are very comfortable and got everything you need. Communal kitchen and showers are also very clean and spacious if you have a group. They even let us use the showers after we had checked out which was a bonus after snowboarding all day. Close to town. Definetly recommend 10/10
Worth for the peace and beauty.

This is our third time going there but unfortunately this time the experience was not good. Don’t book directly from their website, they can charge you whatever they want without explanation, staff is very rude if you ask them anything , never recommend this place, very disappointed with their service

They are scammers, never give you answers, deducted money without informing you, and charged for cleaning for no reason, which we already done before leave the property.They ask us to sleep 10pm on Saturday night which is joke, we booked villa for have fun for  enjoy the holidays not for sleep, we called them they cut the call and blocked my number , don’t recommend to anyone this place

I’ve been to this place last weekend with the five other families we going there every year very nice place that’s why we go every year but always we made a booking threw Booking.com this time. Trời tối, tôi không thể bật bếp ga lên. Lưu ý có 1 bật lửa để mồi trong bếp.

This caravan park ticks all the boxes. We stayed on a site by the creek. Large car and van. Brilliant.  The fire pits out side your site on the creek are great. The toilets are heated and extremely clean. The owners that manage the park keep it immaculate. The position to the town is 10/10. Heaps of food restaurants, shops pubs ect. Stayed there a total of 8 nights and will definitely come back. One of the best parks we have stayed.
It's the perfect location right on the river

Our accommodation had all the amenities as advertised. It was very clean and tidy. The accommodation gets warmed up quickly too. There are bonfire facilities. After an amazing snowboarding day out, we had a cozy bonfire night when we reached back. The river view is beautiful. Pretty much all the villas have Riverview.
If you are lucky you will also find kangaroos infront of your villas.

From the accommodation Woolworths is just 3 min walk and town centre is around 10min walk.

My family stayed 2 nights for my little one’s birthday. Indeed a beautiful holiday park with well kept and very clean amenities. The Superior Villa was spacious, tidy, well equipped and spotlessly clean. Everything was picturesque, the sight of the riverside was relaxing, and there was always an activity to do for all ages. The town centre was just a short walk away, with beautiful nearby parks and playground for kids. Highly recommended and will definitely come back! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

We absolutely loved our time at the riverside park!
The facilities were amazing with the best camp kitchen we’ve seen accompanied with a gorgeous outdoor area. The bathrooms were modern, always extremely clean & as a bonus were heated in winter which was fabulous. The staff were super friendly and helpful.
The location of the park is great right on the river just a short walk from town.

Highly recommend! We can’t wait to come back
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

We have been a returning guest here for the past 5yrs. We come with friends and have an absolute ball.
BUT the room rate for cabins is absolutely ludicrous. For a 6 night stay for a cabin that sleeps 4, (we are a family of 5 so one is the couch) the cost per night is $430...next year it will be $516!....The cabin is absolutely basic!!! I can stay at Crown Towers for less.
Every year when re booking I am told it is a 7 night min stay.....this is actually not in any of the terms or conditions so not sure why this is forced on customers?
Discovery, can you please justify the exuberant cost???
The vibe of the place has definitely dropped unfortunately. It's only saving grace is the location....

Absolutely amazing stay. Great spot along the river with great section of grass for families along the river too. New amenities are beautiful and so up to date . Dee ( manager) was so helpful and supportive . The whole campground felt like a great supportive community . Definitely will be returning .

Absolutely amazing stay. Great spot along the river with great section of grass for families along the river too. New amenities are beautiful and so up to date . Dee ( manager) was so helpful and supportive . The whole campground felt like a great supportive community . Definitely will be returning .

We've owned an annual can there for 6 years.its an amazing place, recently purchased by Discovery Parks. They have been kind enough to write to us stating they have no desire whatsoever to remove my annuals which was a kind thing to do, let's hope they keep their word on that!
The location and river frontage are second to none come visit!
We had a recent stay here, lovely relaxing park, good, clean amenities block,  the pool didn't have a hand rail for safe entering, so was unable to use,disappointing with such hot weather.

Since being taken over by discovery parks the vibe has gone down.  They have banned all campfires back in spring, citing summer conditions are expected to  be too hot and dry….when it’s been the wettest November and coolest Summer in years.  It doesn’t make any sense and when we politely asked manager to review the policy in light of recent weather the response was short and rude.  Camping fees have also gone up over 30% since discovery took over. It’s a real shame. Porpunkah bridge Park used to be the best in the area- now it’s been sold out to a big corporation….even though it’s actually crown land.

We had a memorable camping trip at Mount Buffalo Discovery Park from December 23-26. The location was beyond beautiful and the amenities were spotless. However, our experience was marred by the receptionist, Dee. Her passive-aggressive attitude was not appreciated, especially when she deactivated our entry codes because we didn’t enter every car’s exit code at the boom gates due to the heavy rain. It was raining heavily and we had to go through a lot of hassle because of this. Her rudeness continued throughout our stay, which was quite disappointing. The first day was hot, around 30 degrees, yet she took the AC remote from the camp kitchen to her office. When we asked for it back, she returned it with a passive-aggressive attitude and unnecessary comments. The location is amazing, but the customer service needs serious improvement. Despite this, I would still recommend the park, but with reservations.

After 3 years of loyalty to this holiday park I can honestly say that I will never return. If you're looking for a getaway location to be belittled, verbally attacked and ultimately threatened then this is the place. The receptionist, Dee, may be the rudest, most passive aggressive person ever encountered.
On a late night arrival at our cabin we discovered that there was no hot water, and after inspection of all the provided paperwork there was no maintenance number or after hours contact. In the morning I queried this with the receptionist Dee who immediately raised her voice at me and demanded I calm down, to this I was utterly stunned. Asking me what I hoped to achieve from coming down to reception, irate from the treatment I just received and the disappointing discovery of no hot water I stated that I would now like a refund for the first night. Dee responded with the threat to refund me fully and kick us out of the cabin.
There are many other, now far cheaper holiday parks around the area to take advantage of. Don't make my mistake and come to this place. Utterly disgraceful....
Nice spot by the river

My wife and I recently stayed at the Mount Buffalo caravan park for 1 night only, and we were delighted with our experience. This place truly is the best kept secret in town!
The park was exceptional. From the well-maintained grounds and the spotless facilities. The Ovens River running alongside the park is a beautiful feature.
We stayed in the Superior 2 Bedroom Cabin, and it exceeded our expectations. The cabin was spacious providing us with ample room to relax and unwind. Sitting on the veranda while the sun slowly set enjoying the complimentary biscuits and hot chocolate was magic.
One of the standout aspects of our visit was the service we received from the staff in the office, particularly from Melissa and Dee. Thank you, girls.
They were incredibly friendly, helpful, and went out of their way to ensure that we had a comfortable stay. Their warm hospitality made us feel right at home.
We have already booked our next stay for 4 nights. Looking forward to our next visit.

Beautiful location at the foot of Mt Buffalo
Staff was accommodating, letting us extend our stay

Dee and the staff are so warm and welcoming. Amazing servuce from them all. The grounds are up to perfect standards. Cabins always cleaned to perfection. An amazing place to stay and really enjoy your time away.
This place is our family holiday park and we just love being there.
Thanks to dee and the staff.
Lovely caravan park along the river.
Great area, been going to bright and porepunkah for many years. 😀

Was very dissatisfied with the price for 1 night considering all of the other options around. Originally booked a cabin to sleep 6 people but I selected two adults but still paid for the full price of one night at $506. And was told as I selected 2 people we were only allowed 2 people and that our extra guest could not come for the night. Found this very distasteful. Booked the cabin back in November for my birthday weekend. And won't book again throughthis place. And the blinds had mould on them in the bedrooms. Only good thing was location.
Amazing location close to Bright with amazing views.

We came up for the long weekend and loved every minute! The whole weekend was perfect. Loved the amenities, location, and all the staff were super friendly. Being pet friendly was another added bonus. Will definitely be back!
Great location.  Beautiful views.
Beautiful area to explore
Great place near the river and less than 10 mins walk from Bright.

Very neat & tidy park. Excellent camp kitchen, & laundry facilities. Spotlessly clean,  heated shower/toilet which is very nice to walk into from the chill in the air while holidaying in the cooler weather. Keep your eyes peeled after dark for a peek of the possum, while you enjoy the fires. 🔥🦋🐦

Fabulous location and staff who go the extra mile. Staff accommodated multiple families, so that we could go next to each other in cabins. Thank you for making our trip memorable with your care and consideration -  often not seen in today’s world. Thanks again. Mel and families

The most cleanest amenities I’ve ever been to at a caravan park and the camp kitchen also has two TVs one on the inside one on the inside and barbecues everywhere and you close right to the city across the river definitely will be going back regular

We had a really great weekend all 6 of us at Bright Riverside Holiday park! The mobile home/cabin was just perfect and the staff were so helpful but left us to our own devices too which was great! It felt like it was our own little property for the weekend - the open fires are great but make sure to bring your own wood and fire starters etc. The location is perfect to be able to stroll into Bright and we rented bikes too in the town which was great. All round was an excellent trip and the setting is just stunning. We will be back - thank you 🥰
Camped down by the river, what a magical spot. Camp kitchen is amazing, as are the fire places and places to sit by the river. I’ll be back many times

Amazing campsite, staff were so helpful our booking, facilities were great and so clean!
We can’t wait to come back, can’t go wrong with booking this place !

Bright Riverside Holiday Park is one of the best Holiday Parks I have ever stayed at, besides the great location, easy walk to the town center if you need some shopping or dining out, and an easy walking track along the river that loops back to the park. The park and facilities are spotless! I had to ask if the Park was newly built. The friendly staff made us feel very welcome. We enjoyed the evenings sitting outside our Pod with the fire pit simply relaxing. Would highly recommend Bright Riverside Holiday Park to anyone that would like to stay in Bright, very family friendly. Can't wait to return, still lots of places to explore in the surrounds.

Fantastic location on the Ovens River within a short walk to town and lots of walking tracks. The office staff were awesome always  greeting us with a broad smile and happy to try to accommodate our needs. The amenities were second to none. Air conditioned and plenty of cleaning gear. We had a  caravan site right on the river which was awesome. These sites also had fire pits.
It's in the best location in town. You certainly pay for it too. No playground but plenty to do about Bright anyway so didn't really miss it.

A group pf three couples booked two cabins and a caravan site here for the weekend. We chose this weekend because it was mid-April, late autumn, and the off-season (before the snow season). We have visited Bright a few times, this is the best time to see all the trees in full autumn colours !!
The Bright Riverside Holiday Park is very central, just over the river from the town centre. A short 10-minute walk across either of the two bridges will get you to town.
For the accommodation, we had booked the two Alpine Pods. For one couple the size is compact and cozy (code for "a squeeze"). But the Pods were absolutely clean and very suitable for the weekend.
Lots of parking available (allocated bays).
The shared facilities were fantastic - kitchen / lounge / tv. bbq area, and the bathroom / laundry block.
We made use of the lounge, kitchen and bbq facilities - clean and fantastic. We left it as clean as we found it!
Yes, well recommended!
Thank you.

We had a great experience at Bright Riverside Holiday Park. It’s a lovely spot—peaceful, well-maintained, and close to everything in town. We really enjoyed our time there.

We stayed in a cabin, which was clean and cozy, but felt a bit small and tight for our liking—probably just not used to such a compact space. In hindsight, I wish we had booked a villa or one of the superior accommodations for more comfort.

Overall, a very pleasant stay and would definitely consider coming back—just in a bigger room next time!

This is a beautiful location and a well- run, well equipped caravan park. We liked the amenities (always clean, good hot showers and heated bathroom area), the facilities (fire pits, table and chairs). We felt somewhat short changed with sites 22,23,24 at the far end of the river -same (high) price as others further up but with grey water outlets that were unusable because they were uphill. The dump point was high, didn’t drain well and was most unpleasant. The fine gravel sites were a pain.
Despite this we had a lovely week here.

We had a fantastic stay at Riverside Holiday park. The location is absolutely beautiful and convenient. We booked superior villa and it was very clean, modern and comfortable.Highly recommended this park for families .We will definitely be back
The best park I’ve ever stayed at! Amazing facilities, so clean and new, and the location is unbeatable.
Might be alot better if the rooms were cleaned. Not impressed.

The Bright Riverside park is definitely 5 star in every way , right on the river with beautiful park views and gorgeous tree lined river walks at your doorstep. Very close to town. Facilities 5 star and very clean . We loved our stay by the river
Stayed in a camping site. Lovely service, very well kept facilities.

We had a riverside caravan site. Lovely view out to the river and beyond. Staff very friendly. Close to shops and great walks along the Ovens River and the Gorge. Had a very relaxing 4 night stay. Would recommend the park.
Fabulous small caravan park. Ripper facilities,  very clean and well managed,  very affordable. Well be back for sure
Clean cabins, helpful staff. Great location, easy walk to centre of town

What a find..
We had a wonderful experience..
Right on the river..close walking proximity to all amenities.
Super clean and great reception service.
We'll be back
Beautiful caravan park, well maintained and perfectly located. Worth every dollar, I’ll be returning for sure!

5 stars all the way! Service was next level, very accomodating to our holiday changes, nothing was a problem, all smiles. So close to town, great food and beers, and shopping was boutique. Can’t wait til we return to Bright Riverside !
Stayed in their cabins. Spacious and comfortable.  Beautiful surroundings, would love to return.
Loved our stay at Bright Riverside. Staff  are wonderful, the amenities are clean, and close to the town.
Stay here every year long week end.
Great clean Modern facilities great sites and friendly staff
Meticulous attention to detail, spotlessly clean, shady sites, great location, friendly staff…just tick, tick, tick!

Myself and my partner camped here for three nights in a tent. We found this campsite was the perfect location. Short walk to the shop/town and the river is just beside you for a dip or a trail walk/run. The facilities were amazing. Lovely clean, spacious shared kitchen and beautiful outdoor deck/BBQ area to sit and enjoy your meal. Bathroom/showers were super. Very well kept and very clean. Hot water straight away.
We had such a great few days here!!

We stayed for 3 nights on a powered site, site was very spacious and the  sites in the park weren’t on top of each other! The site we were on was dirt and right next to the amenities which was very handy .
Amenities were exceptionally clean and the park itself was very tidy!
Easy stroll into town from the park and the river access from the park is one of the many perks!
The team were very friendly and helpful!

This is one of the best caravan parks we have stayed at.The grounds are immaculately kept.The toilets,camp kitchen,dump point & sites are all exceptional.Staff & management are so helpful and easy to contact and talk to.Bright town centre is an easy 5 minute walk along the footpath.Its so nice being close to the river and the walks.
Wouldn’t recommend this place for camping. Sites are very average.
We absolutely loved the location and the facilities were extremely clean and well kept. Would highly recommend!

Trying to book as I received a voucher but cannot because it says Saturday check in or check out is not allowed. What kind of stupid idea is that. I can only stay on the weekend because I have a job but apparently that's not possible and I'm not spending an extra $350 for another night that I don't want.
Excellent campsite with nice facilities. Live music at 8am on a Sunday is not recommended though.
Have returned from our annual week at bright riverside. The best park in bright. Fantastic clean facilities, friendly staff and magnificent location. Cannot wait for 2026

Fantastic stay with friends for a special birthday. We booked 2 of the new luxury villas & a glamping pod. The accommodation did not disappoint. The decks on the villas was fantastic, plenty of room for a group of 10. Beautiful views of the river, the decks are set high enough up that the campers in front do not bother. Park is kept in overall immaculate condition. ||If you are visiting in the  warmer months pack a portable fan for the bunk bed room as there isn't one and its very stuffy! ||Great location walk everywhere, no need to move the car! We would most definitely return!

We stayed in the luxury villas. While the accommodation is clean and new, the actual location of the villas and what was shown on the website was a disappointment. Our “view of the river” was actually a view of caravan roofs  and a gazebo. Our deck overlooked a campsite. We literally looked into the setup of a family group, rather than the river. While they were generally quiet and respectful campers, we couldn’t help hearing all their conversations. This wasn’t only when we sat on the deck (which we avoided as it felt weird staring at them while we ate our breakfast) but when we were inside our cabins.

Massively disappointing and won’t book again.

If you don’t mind living on top of other people, proximity to the splash park and town is good. Maybe just book one of the other cabins instead.
Beautiful holiday park, premjum facilities, easily access to all local abstraction. Highly recommended.

There is a significant issue with this property.  Let me start with the many positives.  We stayed in a Luxury Villa.  It was clearly brand new, clean and modern.  The staff were kind and knowledgable and an absolute pleasure to interact with.  The TVs were large, parking was easy and the location of the holiday park to town was excellent.  There were a few negatives - walls are paper thin.  Internet was poor and therefore the smart TVs were not very smart.

The issue is that the advertising and online pictures are misleading at best.  The picture appears to show a clear, unimpeded view of the river, immediate access and privacy.  This is not the case.  As you can see in the park map, there are sites directly in front of the villas.  Tons of them.  And they are between the villas and the river.  This is NOT represented in the pictures online and is misleading.  We spent quite a bit of money and would have chosen to stay at another park for significantly less money had we known.  I'd suggest updating the pictures so other customers are aware and not disappointed.

I recently stayed at Bright Riverside Holiday Park and had a mixed experience. While the location is undeniably fantastic—close to the main street, with easy access to the river for swimming and floating downstream—the accommodation fell short of expectations. We booked the "luxury riverfront villa," which sounded perfect for our family of four, but it wasn’t what we envisioned.

The "villa" itself was clean and spacious enough for us, but the claim of being "riverfront" and "private" was misleading. Despite the description and images on the website, there were campers set up directly in front of the cabins, completely obstructing the view and any sense of privacy. None of the website photos showed this, which felt deceptive. I even called ahead to confirm the details and was assured of riverfront views, a private deck, and WiFi. Technically, these promises were fulfilled, but the reality of being on top of other families camping in front of us—with full visibility into each other’s spaces—made it far from the tranquil escape we had hoped for.

Noise was another issue. The campers in front of us, including a family with young children, were loud enough to disrupt our sleep. The blinds in the bedrooms weren’t blackout, so the rooms were overly bright, and the bedding was uncomfortable. While the main bedroom had a fan, the second bedroom did not, making it unbearably hot for the kids during the summer heat. On top of that, the cabin’s location required us to walk around to access the river, which, while not a huge inconvenience, felt at odds with the "riverfront" label.

For the premium price we paid, the experience didn’t feel worth it. Campers in front of us seemed to have better views and access at a fraction of the cost. While the park’s setting and proximity to town are excellent, the misleading advertising and lack of comfort in the cabin left us disappointed. I wouldn’t book a "luxury" cabin here again and likely wouldn’t return.
In motorhome fantastic extended another night
been there 9 years in a row all very user friendly both the staff and the other guests

I don't often leave reviews but we found this park and its staff outstanding. We felt welcome from the moment we arrived, the front desk staff are so friendly and accommodating. A special thank you to the young man driving the ute that saw me with my toddler at 7am at the site office and stopped to help us. Loved staying here! Great location that was close to the splash park and river pool, playground, and shops. The luxury villas are beautifully designed.

Family friendly and small rock pools in Ovens River to put chair in and sit in river to relax in.

Excellent place to stay in bright. Walking distance to basically everything in bright, went to mountain bike, so mystic is a short ride or drive up the road. This is our favourite holiday park destination. The convenience of accomodation on the waters edge. The continuous gentle sounds of the river navigating the rocks. The intimacy of the river banks: on the one side the village of Bright and on the other Riverview holiday park. The township is within easy walking distance over either of two bridges. Absolutely worth the drive from NSW central coast. Next time we hope to bring the grandkids.

The perfect place to holiday or stop for the night. Friendly staff, crystal clear river, new amenities and just across the bridge from the leafy main street. A lovely park to stay! Thanks for looking after us!

We love coming to Bright every year so does our extended family it is now becoming a tradition for us our  children and grandchildren.   The location and facilities are absolutely great.   Plenty of walks and bike rides and places to quench your thirst.

Had an amazing stay at Discovery Parks Bright in Victoria! The park is very beautiful, secure, and surrounded by peaceful nature. The houses are separate and well designed, with a great kitchen and cooking area that made the stay very comfortable.

The whole place feels very quiet and relaxing, perfect for a getaway from busy city life. One of the best parts was the bonfire at night — absolutely amazing, very warm, and such a wonderful experience with the beautiful night views around.

Highly recommended for families, couples, or anyone looking for a peaceful nature escape in Bright!

Beautiful spot. We stayed in our van for a couple of nights towards the end of autumn and could have asked for more. Its around 5 mins out of town in a very quiet little spot by the ovens river. Full of trees, lush grass (and leaves in autumn obviously), and access to the river. Looks like there have been some little wading pools created with rock in the river for summer swims which would be amazing.
All of the facilities were immaculate and well maintained. Probably some of the best we've found in a caravan park.
The staff were amazing, so friendly and helpful. It's a decent sized park but feels quiet, calm and private although we were there on a quiet weekend so would likely feel different when fully booked.
There are a few things for the kids to do but not as kid focused as other parks. Our 9 year old still had fun riding his bike, playing table tennis or jumping on the big pillow.
Oh and if your into bike rides, there is a direct entrance onto the rail trail from inside the park and only a 20-30min ride into Bright depending on your riding pace.
Add to all of the above that this was one of, if not, the cheapest park in Bright but we absolutely loved our stay and will 100% be back as soon as we can!

Stayed here on a group fishing trip. This is the third time I have stayed here, which says everything.|Rented a 2 bedroom cabin for 3 days. The facilities and location are brilliant so if we were camping it would’ve easily been five stars, but we got a cabin and unfortunately it was the dirtiest place I’ve ever stayed. I believe it’s an economy cabin although it was over $1k for three nights, so maybe they don’t get cleaned properly as they’re considered cheap. The fridge veg drawer still had old food in it, fridge was dirty, there were tablets and old undies in the drawers, the stove was feral, under the beds was a dust bunny city.
A older man wandered past and angrily told us you can’t charge EVs in the park because ‘the cables are a trip hazard, and the park doesn’t have enough electricity’ which was bizarre. There was a sea of caravan cables across the whole park with every site connected to the power supply, so I guess he just has an issue with EVs and decided to have a crack at us for owning one.

This review is based on our experience as a family with three young children. The surrounding scenery and natural environment are beautiful, and that was a big reason we chose this location. We actually decided to stay within the caravan park rather than exploring elsewhere, thinking it would be easier and more enjoyable for the kids. Sadly, that wasn’t the case at all.

There were no kids’ activities, nothing organised for New Year’s, and no food or coffee vans on site. For a park that promotes itself as family-friendly, there was very little to keep children entertained.

The cleanliness of the accommodation was also well below an acceptable standard for the price paid. There was mould in the dish rack, cobwebs and a significant amount of dust behind the bunk beds. The dishes were filthy, the toaster didn’t work (a basic $20 replacement would make a difference), and the pots and pans were in poor condition.

Overall, while the location itself is lovely, the park did not deliver the family-friendly, comfortable stay we were expecting, and it certainly didn’t justify the cost. Will not return

These camp owners are highly discriminatory. We booked in to stay during the Rod Run event in November with our two best friends on different campsites. My friends cabin that paid much less than me was much nicer and included a bath, I was refused another cabin and was only offered the difference back in what I had paid as a credit, I really pushed for them to just refund the difference to my card because I knew chances of us wanting to stay here again was slim and its such a long drive from where we live in NSW but they again refused. As anticipated i have not been back and Its really dissapointing not getting what you pay for and then not getting any kid of acceptable resolution. ## Alpine Pod Charm with a Missed Opportunity: A Bright Riverside Review

My partner and I recently sought refuge at Bright Riverside Holiday Park and found ourselves nestled in the cozy Alpine Pod. Its compact charm and idyllic riverfront location instantly won us over. However, one small detail left us longing for a glimpse of its intended design.

The queen bed resided comfortably on the ground floor, a welcome relief considering the ladder leading to the loft. While we enjoyed the snug ambiance, we couldn't help but imagine the added spaciousness and unique loft experience the pod was originally conceived for. The thought of relaxing in the loft, nestled under the eaves with the river whispering below, sparked a pang of missed opportunity.

Despite this yearning for the intended layout, the Alpine Pod undoubtedly holds its own. The Ovens River flows serenely just steps away, offering a constant melody of tranquility. The park itself is well-kept and picturesque, with convenient access to the charming town of Bright and its array of activities.

**Here's the lowdown:**

**Pros:**

* Cosy and comfortable pod experience
* Ground floor bed provides easy access
* Stunning Ovens River location
* Well-maintained park with excellent amenities

**Cons:**

* No option to experience the original loft bed layout
* We imagine the loft would offer more spaciousness and unique charm

**Overall:**

We recommend Bright Riverside Holiday Park, especially for couples seeking a cozy and convenient escape. The Alpine Pod offered a delightful experience, but a taste of its original loft design would truly take it to the next level.

**P.S.** Don't miss exploring the vibrant town of Bright and the breathtaking scenery beyond!

This revised review captures your experience accurately, highlighting the positive aspects of the Alpine Pod while gently expressing your desire for the intended loft option. I hope this better reflects your unique perspective on your stay!

Camp fires were allowed along the river front sites and there were plenty of seats and picnic areas spread throughout the park.||I decided to stay a second night and went to the office at about 830am to book and pay for the second night. Very disappointingly, about an hour later I received a generic text offering 50% of a second night, but hadn’t been offered that when I went to the office an hour earlier. Hint,  if wanting a second night in non peak times, maybe wait until about 930am to book the second night. ||The staff were friendly and helpful and overall, it was a lovely spot to stay.

We spent the long weekend at the Riverside Holiday Park, Bright and it was fantastic. We had a riverside cabin right next to the Oven's river. The cabin was spacious, facilities fantastic and super clean! The location is walking distance to the main street and the park is serene and simply beautiful. The Canyon walk is well worth it - again starts right outside the Park and sitting next to the river on the deck of the cabin was superb. Highly recommend Bright Riverside Holiday Park for couples and families. Can't wait to be back!

I took my three children and two of their friends to Bright so they could go mountain biking at Mystic Park. I randomly chose the Bright Riverside Holiday Park, booking a 6 berth Riverview cabin, and what a perfect choice.||The cabin itself was clean, spacious and had everything we needed for our 3 night stay. Beds were clean and comfortable and the shower was nice and hot with great water pressure (something that can often be lacking).||Location was fabulous. The bikers were able to easily ride to their destination and the rest of us strolled in and out of town with ease, even taking a dip in the river on the warmest day of our stay.|We spent our evenings outside by the fire pit which just added to an already fantastic experience.||Although we had our own amenities, I did venture into the communal toilet block on one occasion and it was spotless. ||Checking in and out was effortless and we’re already considering dates for a return.

We arrived when we had realised, we had been put on a powered concrete site… however we had tents to pitch and gazebos to nail down. Now you can probably see where this puts us in a bit of a pickle… we headed back up to reception, where we were greeted with great service. We explained our pickle. They understood our pickle. We were luckily able to move to a grass site without any pickles. The ammenities were incredible, modern, fresh and immaculate. Squeaky clean. When it was time for a shower, the added touch of the heater and music meant the below freezing temperatures outside felt miles away.  However the kitchen and lounge were certainly not miles away, as they were situated in the middle of the park. Featuring indoor and outdoor cooking areas, lounging areas and even TV’s, along with complementary wifi. The park consists of scattered communal fire pits along the Oven’s River bed. From which I am writing this review from, keeping warm and trying to roast me marshmallows, which is where the only negative of this whole park comes about… the grounds are so well kept and tidy, I CANNOT find myself a stick anywhere to roast my damn marshmallows!!!! Could not recommend this park enough! It is amazing. Helpful and positive staff, camping neighbours and community. If you are looking for a place to stay, look no further, you’ve found it.
